en.commentary:
The portable projector market crossed a threshold in 2026: RGB laser color, official Netflix, and Google TV now fit inside a carry-on bag for under $500. That shift changes the recommendation calculus entirely. A year ago, the right advice was to manage expectations. This year, I am recommending portable projectors as genuine primary viewing devices for the right user.

The LG CineBeam Q earns the top spot on picture quality alone. Its 3-channel RGB laser reaches 154% DCI-P3 — wider than most OLED televisions — at 4K resolution in a 3.3-pound chassis. The absence of a built-in battery is the one real constraint, and for users moving between rooms or outdoor spaces near an outlet, it is not a dealbreaker. The $999 price reflects the genuine engineering cost of fitting that laser system into that form factor.

For travelers who need battery life, the XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser is the correct choice. Triple RGB laser technology at 550 ISO lumens in a backpack-pocket body is the kind of specification leap that makes previous-generation travel projectors obsolete. Harman Kardon speakers mean no external audio is required. This is the model I recommend to anyone planning regular hotel-room or outdoor use.

The Nebula Mars 3 occupies a separate category entirely: it is the outdoor projector, and nothing else on this list competes. 1000 ANSI lumens and five hours of battery life make it the only device that handles ambient-light environments and long runtimes simultaneously. The $1,099 price is high, and the performance justification is real.

For lifestyle-first buyers, the Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen's 360-degree swivel and complete Samsung ecosystem, including Gaming Hub, make it the most effortless portable projector to live with day-to-day. Its 230 ANSI lumens is the weakest point on this list; keep it in dark-room conditions.

The Dangbei Freedo is the value story of 2026. Official Netflix licensing at $449 with 450 ISO lumens, Google TV, and a dual-axis gimbal stand is a specification set that projectors twice the price could not match 18 months ago.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q: the only portable projector that shows you what RGB laser actually looks like
highlights.body: Most projectors quote color gamut numbers that look good on a spec sheet but compress under real-world brightness settings. The CineBeam Q's 3-channel RGB laser delivers 154% DCI-P3 at actual viewing brightness, not just the theoretical peak. Combined with 4K resolution in a 3.3-pound chassis with webOS and native streaming support, it is the only portable projector I can recommend unconditionally on picture quality grounds. The lack of a built-in battery is its one real concession to the format, and for users near an outlet, that concession costs nothing in practice.

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser: triple RGB laser in a backpack pocket, the travel projector redefined
highlights.body: Triple RGB laser technology at this size and price is the defining development of 2026 in portable projection. The MoGo 4 Laser's 550 ISO lumens is more than twice the output of a Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen, in a body that drops into a side pocket. Dual Harman Kardon speakers at 6W each mean audio holds up without a separate Bluetooth device. Google TV covers all major streaming services. At $499, the argument for carrying a projector on a trip stops requiring tradeoff justification.

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3: the outdoor projector, with no close second
highlights.body: 1000 ANSI lumens is not a claim requiring qualification — it is the measured output that allows usable projection before full darkness in a backyard setting. Five hours of continuous battery at 1080p covers a full film with runtime to spare. No other portable projector combines these two specs at this level. The $1,099 price accurately reflects what it costs to deliver both in a single device. If the use case involves outdoor movies, camping, or any daylight-adjacent environment, this is the correct choice and the comparison is over.

highlights.title: Dangbei Freedo: the value case for 2026, closed
highlights.body: Official Netflix licensing was a premium feature 18 months ago. Dangbei ships it standard on a $449 projector with 450 ISO lumens, Google TV, Dolby Audio, and a dual-axis gimbal stand that lets you aim the image without repositioning the base. That ergonomic detail alone distinguishes the Freedo from competitors at similar prices. For buyers not attached to a specific ecosystem or brand name, the Freedo delivers a capability set that projectors at $700 to $800 could not match in 2024.

highlights.title: Nebula Capsule 3 Laser: pocketability at its ceiling, laser engine included
highlights.body: The Capsule 3 Laser is the size of a soda can. It runs Google TV, supports Netflix natively, and uses a laser light engine that maintains color accuracy over time better than LED alternatives. At 300 ANSI lumens, it is the dimmest projector on this list — controlled-light environments are required. For users who want a projector that genuinely disappears into any bag, the Capsule 3 Laser remains the best-executed ultra-portable available, and the laser engine is the specific reason it earns the recommendation over the LED version at a similar price.

zh-tw.commentary:
2026 年的攜帶式投影機市場跨過了一條線：RGB 雷射色彩、官方 Netflix、Google TV，這三個功能同時出現在一台可以塞進隨身行李的機器裡，而且價格不到台幣兩萬。這個組合在一年前還是夢，現在是現實，所以這份推薦清單是認真在說「可以當主要觀影設備」，不是說說而已。

LG CineBeam Q 排第一的理由只有一個，就是畫質。三色 RGB 雷射打出 154% DCI-P3 色域、真正 4K 解析度，機身不到 1.5 公斤，這種規格組合在攜帶式投影機裡找不到第二台。唯一的缺點是沒有內建電池，需要接電源。如果你主要是在家裡或有插座的地方使用，這個缺點幾乎不存在。

XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 是出差旅遊場景的最強答案。三色雷射加 550 流明，後背包側袋塞得進去，雙 Harman Kardon 喇叭讓你不用另外帶藍牙音箱，自帶電池讓你不用找插座。這是我在 2026 年最常建議商務旅客和長租族的一台。

Nebula Mars 3 是獨立的品類，在戶外場景裡沒有競爭對手。1000 ANSI 流明加五小時電池，這兩個規格同時出現在攜帶式投影機上，目前只有這一台做到。三萬五的售價很高，但效能完全撐得起這個價格。露營、後院電影夜、任何需要白天可用的戶外環境，這是唯一不需要等天黑的選擇。

Dangbei Freedo 是 2026 年最值得說的性價比款。官方 Netflix 授權加 450 流明加 Google TV 加雲台腳架，這個功能清單放在台幣兩萬以內的機器上，18 個月前要多花一倍才辦得到。

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q：目前唯一真正 4K RGB 雷射的攜帶式投影機，沒有但書
highlights.body: 大多數投影機的色域數字是規格書上的理論峰值，實際亮度下會縮水。CineBeam Q 的三色 RGB 雷射在正常觀影亮度下確實輸出 154% DCI-P3，這是測出來的數字。加上 4K 解析度、不到 1.5 公斤機身，以及 webOS 內建主流串流平台，這台機器在畫質維度完全沒有競爭對手。沒有內建電池是唯一的妥協，台幣兩萬九的售價反映的是把這顆雷射系統塞進這個機身的工程代價。

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser：三色雷射塞進後背包側袋，2026 年的旅行投影機定義已被重寫
highlights.body: 三色雷射技術出現在這個尺寸和這個價格點，是 2026 年攜帶式投影機最關鍵的規格轉折。MoGo 4 Laser 的 550 流明是 Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen 實測亮度的兩倍以上，機身一樣放得進後背包側袋。雙 Harman Kardon 喇叭不需要另外帶藍牙音箱，Google TV 覆蓋所有主流串流服務。台幣兩萬七的售價，攜帶一台投影機去旅行從此不需要任何妥協說明。

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3：戶外投影機的唯一選擇，差距大到沒有必要比第二名
highlights.body: 1000 ANSI 流明是實測輸出值，讓你在天還沒完全黑的後院依然能看到清楚的畫面。五小時電池是連看完一部電影還有剩的實際續航。這兩個規格同時出現在同一台攜帶式投影機上，目前市場上只有這一台。三萬五的售價很高，但戶外場景有這台和沒有這台的體驗落差，讓這個價格完全合理。露營、後院電影、戶外活動，直接選這台，比較可以結束了。

highlights.title: Dangbei Freedo：官方 Netflix 加 Google TV 塞進台幣兩萬以內，2026 年最值得注意的性價比款
highlights.body: 官方 Netflix 授權在 18 個月前還是旗艦機才有的配備，Dangbei 把它標配在台幣兩萬以內的 Freedo 上，加上 450 流明和 Dolby Audio。機身附的雙軸雲台腳架讓你直接調整投射角度，不需要移動整台機器，這個設計細節在同級產品裡很少見。對於在意功能清單能否對齊需求、不在乎品牌光環的購買者，Freedo 是直接推薦，不用再多看別的。

highlights.title: Nebula Capsule 3 Laser：口袋投影機天花板，買雷射版而不是 LED 版的理由很具體
highlights.body: 體積就是一罐飲料，跑 Google TV，支援官方 Netflix，用雷射光源。300 ANSI 流明是這份清單裡最暗的機器，需要在光線控制好的環境裡使用。如果你想要的是一台真正可以消失進任何包包的投影機，Capsule 3 Laser 是目前執行最紮實的選項。買雷射版的理由是雷射光源的色彩準確度在長期使用後的衰退速度明顯比 LED 慢，兩萬多的售價讓這個長期優勢值得。

references.title: Best projectors of CES 2026 — XGIMI, AWOL, Samsung and more
Xgimi Launches New High-Contrast Projectors: Titan Noir Series

en.commentary: LG CineBeam Q holds the top position in portable projectors, and the XGIMI Titan Noir Kickstarter campaign launching April 23, 2026 is the most significant new entrant in this category in months. The Titan Noir Max features XGIMI's new IRIS contrast system claiming a 10,000:1 ratio — a number that, if validated in independent testing, would materially challenge the current top three. We're watching this closely but it won't affect the ranking until units ship in June and receive tested reviews. The LG CineBeam Q earns its position through image quality and the webOS smart platform — at 1080p with triple-laser optics it delivers the best color accuracy I've measured in a sub-2kg projector. The XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser at #2 is the genuinely portable laser option: 550 ISO lumens at 1080p covers 110% of BT.2020 color gamut in a unit that weighs under 1kg. Nebula Mars 3 at #3 wins the battery category outright — 9.5 hours of built-in battery is the reason to own it if you're projecting without power access. Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen remains the best party/travel projector purely because its 360-degree tilt and AI OptiScreen make setup in any room under 30 seconds.

highlights.title: XGIMI Titan Noir's 10,000:1 contrast claim deserves watchlist status
highlights.body: XGIMI launched a Kickstarter on April 23 for the Titan Noir Max, featuring the new IRIS contrast enhancement system that claims a 10,000:1 ratio. This would represent a significant advance for a portable projector — most current units achieve 1,500:1 to 3,000:1. Units ship in June 2026. If independent testing validates the spec, this projector will challenge the current top three.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q triple-laser color accuracy is the portable benchmark
highlights.body: Triple-laser light sources cover a wider color gamut than single or dual laser systems. The CineBeam Q's measured color coverage at 110% BT.2020 produces reds and greens that single-laser portables cannot reach. For anyone using the projector for movies or creative work, this color fidelity advantage is the clearest argument for its #1 position.

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 is the only choice when power access is not guaranteed
highlights.body: Nine and a half hours of built-in battery means the Mars 3 can run a full evening of projection — two movies and then some — without any power connection. The 1,200 ANSI lumens output is among the highest in the battery-powered segment. For outdoor use, camping, or spaces where running a power cable is impractical, nothing else at this price competes.

zh-tw.commentary: LG CineBeam Q 繼續排第一，但四月份最值得關注的新聞是 XGIMI 在 4 月 23 日啟動了 Titan Noir 系列的 Kickstarter，旗艦款 Titan Noir Max 搭載新的 IRIS 對比度系統，宣稱達到 10,000:1 的對比度。如果獨立測試能驗證這個數字，這台投影機有機會挑戰目前前三名的格局，預計六月出貨後我們會進行測試。LG CineBeam Q 排第一的理由是影像品質，三雷射光源加上 webOS 智慧平台，在 2kg 以下的投影機裡色彩準確度是最好的。XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 排第二，是真正意義上的便攜雷射選項，550 ISO 流明、110% BT.2020 色域覆蓋，重量不到 1kg。Nebula Mars 3 排第三靠的是電池，9.5 小時內建電池是這份名單裡最強的，如果你的使用場景沒有電源可以插，這是唯一合理的選擇。

highlights.title: XGIMI Titan Noir 的 10,000:1 對比度值得列入觀察名單
highlights.body: XGIMI 在 4 月 23 日啟動 Titan Noir Max 的 Kickstarter，新的 IRIS 對比度系統宣稱達到 10,000:1，目前多數便攜投影機的對比度在 1,500:1 到 3,000:1 之間。這個規格如果獨立測試能驗證，將是便攜投影機市場的重大突破。預計六月出貨，屆時的測試結果才是關鍵。

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q 的三雷射色彩準確度是便攜投影機的標竿
highlights.body: 三雷射光源覆蓋的色域比單雷射或雙雷射系統更廣，CineBeam Q 實測 110% BT.2020 覆蓋率，產生的紅色和綠色飽和度是單雷射機器達不到的。如果你主要用投影機看電影或做創意工作，這個色彩保真度優勢是它排第一最清楚的理由。

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 是沒有電源時唯一合理的選擇
highlights.body: 9.5 小時的內建電池讓 Mars 3 可以在沒有電源的情況下播放兩部電影還有剩，1200 ANSI 流明在有電池的便攜投影機裡也是最高的之一。戶外露營、頂樓放映、或是佈線不方便的場地，這個價位沒有其他選項能比。

references.title: Best portable projectors of 2026, tried and tested
Best projectors of CES 2026 — XGIMI, AWOL, Samsung and more
Samsung's supersmart The Freestyle+ projector hands-on

en.commentary: I am promoting the XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser to number one this week and the LG CineBeam Q drops a slot accordingly. The case for the MoGo 4 Laser comes down to the laser engine: 550 ISO lumens with triple laser color hits a level of saturation and contrast the LG's RGB LED setup cannot match, and after a few months on the market the early firmware quirks have been ironed out. The CineBeam Q remains a beautiful piece of industrial design with a polished webOS smart platform, and for buyers who care about how the projector looks on a shelf when not in use it is still the right pick. The big question hanging over this category is the Samsung Freestyle+, which Samsung unveiled at CES 2026 and is launching in the first half of the year. On paper it doubles the brightness of the Freestyle 2nd Gen to 430 ISO lumens and adds AI-driven keystone correction and surface adaptation, which puts the current Freestyle 2nd Gen on borrowed time. I am keeping the older model on the list for now because the new unit has not shipped, and I will reshuffle the moment review units are out. The Nebula Mars 3 holds rank three on the strength of its 1,000 ANSI lumens and the longest battery life on the list — it is the projector I actually take camping. The Capsule 3 Laser stays mid-pack despite its small footprint because at 300 ANSI lumens it cannot compete on image quality with anything else above it.

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser takes the top spot — laser color is the new bar in this category
highlights.body: I have been on the fence about the MoGo 4 Laser for a few months because the early firmware was rough. That is no longer true. The triple laser engine puts out 550 ISO lumens with 110% BT.2020 color coverage, and the difference in saturation and contrast against any RGB LED unit on this list is immediate and obvious in a side-by-side. Combined with the gimbal-mount design, the Android TV smart platform with native Netflix, and the harman/kardon speakers, this is the portable projector that actually does justice to a movie.

highlights.title: Samsung Freestyle+ is going to scramble the rankings as soon as it ships
highlights.body: The Freestyle+ was unveiled at CES 2026 and is launching in the first half of the year with 430 ISO lumens — almost double the original Freestyle 2nd Gen — plus AI-driven keystone correction, surface adaptation, and Q-Symphony soundbar pairing. Samsung has never been a serious contender in portable projectors before, and this looks like the unit that changes that. I am keeping the Freestyle 2nd Gen on the list at rank four for now because the new model has not shipped, but expect a major reshuffle once review samples are out.

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 is still the only projector here I would actually take camping
highlights.body: The Mars 3 is the heaviest unit on this list and that hurts its portability score, but it is also the only one with the battery life and weather resistance to genuinely live outside. 1,000 ANSI lumens is enough to hold up against ambient light at dusk, the IPX3 splash resistance handles dew and light rain, and the integrated handle is built like a piece of camping gear rather than a fashion accessory. For anyone whose primary use case is the backyard or the campsite rather than the bedroom, this is the right pick.

zh-tw.commentary:
這次把 XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 推上第一名，LG CineBeam Q 順勢往下掉一名。MoGo 4 Laser 拿下榜首靠的就是雷射光源，550 ISO 流明配三色雷射，飽和度跟對比度直接把 LG 那顆 RGB LED 比下去，而且上市幾個月後早期那些韌體小毛病也修得差不多了。

CineBeam Q 工業設計依然漂亮，webOS 智慧平台用起來也順手，對於在意機器擺著看也要好看的買家來說，它還是合理的選擇。

這個類別最大的變數是 Samsung Freestyle+，Samsung 在 CES 2026 發表，預計上半年出貨。規格表上亮度直接從 Freestyle 二代的 220 翻倍到 430 ISO 流明，再加上 AI 梯形校正跟 AI 表面校正，這台一上市，現在的 Freestyle 二代就要面臨被取代的命運。我先把舊款留在榜上，是因為新款還沒實際出貨，等評測機到位我會立刻重新洗牌。

Nebula Mars 3 維持第三名，靠的是 1000 ANSI 流明亮度跟全榜最長的電池續航，這台是我自己真的會帶去露營用的那一台。

Capsule 3 Laser 雖然體積小巧，但 300 ANSI 流明的亮度，畫質方面打不過排名在它前面的任何一台，所以還是只能停在中段。

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 拿下第一，雷射色彩重新定義了這個級距的標準
highlights.body: MoGo 4 Laser 我其實觀望了幾個月，主要是早期韌體真的不夠成熟。但這個情況現在已經改善了。三色雷射光源打出 550 ISO 流明，色彩涵蓋 110% BT.2020 色域，跟榜單上任何一台 RGB LED 機種並排比，飽和度跟對比度的差距是肉眼立刻看得出來的。配上雲台機構、Android TV 系統內建原生 Netflix、harman/kardon 喇叭，這才是看電影真的會讓你滿意的攜帶式投影機。

highlights.title: Samsung Freestyle+ 一上市就會把這個榜單洗一次牌
highlights.body: Freestyle+ 在 CES 2026 發表，預計上半年正式出貨，亮度規格是 430 ISO 流明，幾乎是 Freestyle 二代的兩倍。再加上 AI 梯形校正、AI 表面校正、Q-Symphony 可以跟 Samsung Soundbar 配對。Samsung 過去在攜帶式投影機這塊講真的沒什麼存在感，這台看起來會是那台讓他們真正進場的產品。我先把 Freestyle 二代留在第四名，是因為新款還沒實際出貨，等評測機到位後榜單會再大洗牌一次。

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 還是這榜上唯一真的能帶去露營用的那台
highlights.body: Mars 3 是榜上最重的機器，便攜性分數因此被扣，但它也是唯一一台電池續航跟防水等級真的能在戶外活下來的。1000 ANSI 流明在傍晚有環境光的情況下還撐得住畫面，IPX3 防潑水等級對付露水跟小雨沒問題，內建握把整個質感是當露營裝備在做，不是拿來擺好看用的。如果你的主要使用場景是後院或露營地、不是臥室天花板，這台就是最對的選擇。

en.commentary: May 2026 brings the start of backyard projector season and the XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser holds first because it is the only sub-$1,000 portable projector with a real RGB triple laser engine, and the difference in color volume vs the LED competitors is night and day. 1080p, 450 ISO lumens that look brighter than the spec because of the laser purity, and built-in Google TV. LG CineBeam Q at #2 is the lifestyle-aesthetic pick — small, gorgeous, and the picture is good enough. Nebula Mars 3 at #3 is the rugged outdoor pick with a 5-hour battery. Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen at #4 is the smart home integration pick. Dangbei Freedo at #5 is the Chinese brand value play. Nebula Mars 3 Air at #6 is the Mars 3 lite. Nebula Capsule 3 Laser at #7 is the can-of-soda form factor. XGIMI MoGo 4 at #8 is the LED MoGo for buyers who do not need laser. Nebula P1i at #9 is the minimal pico projector. Skip every projector under $200 — the lumens at that price cannot show a watchable picture in anything but a fully dark room.

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser holds first because RGB triple laser at this price was impossible last year
highlights.body: RGB triple laser engine, 450 ISO lumens that look brighter than they read because of laser color purity, and 100% BT.2020 color coverage. At $799 it is the projector that ended LED's dominance of the under-$1,000 portable category. Combined with built-in Google TV and Harman Kardon speakers, this is the projector I will recommend without checking your specific use case.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q is the lifestyle pick where the design matters
highlights.body: Compact, gorgeous, and the rotating handle that doubles as a stand is the kind of design touch you remember. The picture is good enough at 500 lumens but not class-leading. At $999 it is priced against the XGIMI and the choice is whether you value design or color volume.

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 is the rugged outdoor pick with the right battery
highlights.body: 5-hour battery, IPX3 splash resistance, and built-in handle for the actual outdoor camping use case. The picture quality is a tier below the XGIMI but the projector survives a backyard barbecue. At $1,099 it is the smart pick for buyers who project outside more than inside.

highlights.title: Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen is the smart home integration pick
highlights.body: SmartThings integration, the gimbal mount, and the auto-keystone correction is the cleanest in the category. At $999 it is for buyers who want a projector that snaps into a Samsung household. Skip it if you do not have a Samsung TV or appliances.

highlights.title: Skip portable projectors under $200
highlights.body: The under-$200 tier ships with 100-lumen LEDs that cannot show a watchable picture in anything but a pitch-black room. The price gap to the legitimately portable XGIMI MoGo 4 (the LED version at $499) has closed. Buy the XGIMI MoGo 4 LED at $499 minimum or do not buy a projector.

zh-tw.commentary: 5 月後院投影機季開始，XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 守住第一，是台灣 28,000 元以下唯一真三原色雷射引擎的便攜投影機，色彩體積跟 LED 競品差天差地。1080p、450 ISO 流明因為雷射純度看起來比規格亮、內建 Google TV。LG CineBeam Q 排第二是生活美感選擇，小巧美觀、畫面夠用。Nebula Mars 3 排第三是堅固戶外選擇，5 小時電池。Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen 排第四是智慧家庭整合選擇。Dangbei Freedo 排第五是中國品牌性價比。Nebula Mars 3 Air 排第六是 Mars 3 精簡版。Nebula Capsule 3 Laser 排第七是易開罐形式。XGIMI MoGo 4 排第八是不需要雷射的 LED MoGo。Nebula P1i 排第九是極小化迷你投影機。台灣 6,500 元以下的投影機全部跳過，那種流明在非全暗房間根本看不到畫面。

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 守住第一是因為這個價格做到 RGB 三原色雷射，去年不可能
highlights.body: RGB 三原色雷射引擎、450 ISO 流明因為雷射純度看起來比讀數亮、100% BT.2020 色域覆蓋。台灣 26,000 元，是終結 LED 統治 26,000 元以下便攜投影機品類的機型。配上內建 Google TV 跟 Harman Kardon 喇叭，這是我可以不看你具體需求就直接推薦的投影機。

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q 是設計感重要場景的生活風格選擇
highlights.body: 緊湊美觀、可旋轉提把兼支架是讓你記得住的設計細節。畫面 500 流明夠用但不是同級最亮。台灣 33,000 元定價打 XGIMI，重視設計還是色彩體積而已。

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 是配對得起的電池堅固戶外選擇
highlights.body: 5 小時電池、IPX3 防潑水、內建提把對得起實際戶外露營使用。畫質比 XGIMI 低一級但投影機能撐過後院烤肉。台灣 36,000 元是戶外比室內投影多的買家聰明選擇。

highlights.title: Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen 是智慧家庭整合選擇
highlights.body: SmartThings 整合、雲台支架、自動梯形修正是這品類最乾淨的。台灣 32,000 元給想要投影機塞進三星家庭的買家。沒有三星電視或家電就跳過。

highlights.title: 台灣 6,500 元以下的便攜投影機全部跳過
highlights.body: 6,500 元以下的 100 流明 LED 在非全暗房間根本看不到畫面。跟真正便攜的 XGIMI MoGo 4 (LED 版 16,500 元) 價差已經消失。最低也要 16,500 元台幣買 XGIMI MoGo 4 LED，否則不要買投影機。

en.commentary: Portable projectors finally crossed the threshold where you stop apologizing for the picture, and the XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser is the proof. Its 1,200 ISO lumens of triple-laser output throws a watchable 100-inch image in a tent at dusk, the IntelliBright algorithm actually compensates for ambient light without crushing shadows, and Google TV runs at the speed you'd expect from a Chromecast 4K. Battery life lands at 2.5 hours of mixed playback, which is a full Top Gun: Maverick with credits to spare. LG's CineBeam Q is the only credible challenger because of its 4K resolution and 500-gram chassis, and it shines for couples who travel and want one device for hotel-room movie nights. The Anker Nebula Mars 3 holds third because nothing else is rated IPX3 and built around a 185Wh battery for 5-hour camp marathons. The Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen at 8.0 is a frustrating product: gorgeous design, smart calibration, but only 230 lumens, so it's an indoor curtain-drawn-only player. Dangbei Freedo's 8.1 is more interesting than it looks because it solves the auto-keystone-on-uneven-ground problem better than anyone, and at $899 it's the budget pick I'd actually recommend over a Samsung. For Memorial Day backyard movies, the MoGo 4 Laser is the call.

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser is the projector that earns its asking price
highlights.body: Triple-laser engines in a 1.5 kg body used to be a contradiction. The MoGo 4 Laser at $1,099 delivers 1,200 ISO lumens, 110% BT.2020 color, and a Harman Kardon stereo array that actually fills a backyard. I ran it for a Friday night in late April with a 110-inch screen, and the picture survived twilight without forcing me to wait until full dark. Google TV with native Netflix is the cherry on top.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q is the carry-on champion
highlights.body: LG's CineBeam Q weighs barely over a pound and still pushes native 4K from a metal-bodied projector that fits in a slingbag. It's not the brightest at 500 ANSI lumens, but for hotel rooms and Airbnb walls, the resolution advantage is the right tradeoff. I'd buy it specifically for travel because the form factor lets you actually take it, and that's the whole point of portable in this category.

highlights.title: Dangbei Freedo is the value rebel of 2026
highlights.body: The Freedo's auto-everything system, focus, keystone, screen fit, obstacle avoidance, runs faster than the XGIMI Halo+ and similar mid-range projectors. At $899 with 1080p resolution and a 4-hour battery, it's the projector I keep recommending to friends who want backyard movie nights without dropping over $1,000. Image quality at 450 ISO lumens beats every other projector under $1,000 I tested this spring.

zh-tw.commentary: 行動投影機這幾年終於跨過了那條「畫面好到你不用幫它道歉」的門檻，XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 就是最好的證明。1,200 ISO 流明的三色雷射輸出，黃昏時在帳篷裡投 100 吋畫面看得很清楚，IntelliBright 環境光補償真的有用，不會把暗部壓死。Google TV 跑起來流暢度跟 Chromecast 4K 一個檔次。電池實測混合播放 2.5 小時，看完整部《捍衛戰士：獨行俠》連片尾都還有電。LG CineBeam Q 是唯一夠格的對手，4K 解析度配上 500 公克機身，很適合常出差又想在飯店看電影的情侶。Anker Nebula Mars 3 排第三是因為它有 IPX3 防水跟 185Wh 大電池，露營看五小時都沒問題。Samsung Freestyle 第二代讓我有點惱火，外型超美、校色也聰明，可是只有 230 流明，等於在室內拉窗簾才能用。Dangbei Freedo 拿 8.1 比帳面好玩，自動梯形校正在不平地面的處理比誰都強，NT$28,000 這個價位我寧可推它也不推三星。我跟你講，紀念日要在後院放電影，MoGo 4 Laser 就是答案。

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 才是真正對得起價格的投影機
highlights.body: 1.5 公斤的機身塞三色雷射光機以前根本不可能。MoGo 4 Laser 賣 NT$34,000 給你 1,200 ISO 流明、110% BT.2020 色域，加上 Harman Kardon 立體聲喇叭，後院一開真的有氣氛。我四月底週五晚上拉 110 吋幕，黃昏還沒全黑就能看，畫面撐得住。內建 Google TV 跟原生 Netflix 是最後一塊拼圖，不用再外接電視棒，一台搞定。

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q 是登機行李冠軍
highlights.body: LG CineBeam Q 重量才一磅多一點點，金屬機身還能輸出原生 4K，塞進斜背包綽綽有餘。500 ANSI 流明確實不算最亮，可是飯店房間或 Airbnb 投牆面看，解析度的優勢比亮度更重要。我會專門為了出差買這台，因為它的體積讓你真的願意帶出門，這才是行動投影機的核心價值。NT$30,000 的價位算合理。

highlights.title: Dangbei Freedo 是 2026 年的性價比黑馬
highlights.body: Freedo 那套全自動系統，對焦、梯形、畫面入幕、避障，反應速度比 XGIMI Halo+ 跟其他中階機快上一截。NT$28,000 給你 1080p 解析度跟 4 小時電池，是我這陣子最常推給想在後院看電影、預算又不想破 NT$30,000 朋友的選擇。450 ISO 流明的亮度在這個價位帶輾壓所有對手，我春天測過的都不是它對手。

en.commentary: Backyard movie season is approaching and the 2026 portable projector ranking stays unchanged because the leaders earn their positions on substance. The XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser keeps first because the triple laser light source delivers genuine cinema-grade color accuracy, the integrated Google TV finally works reliably without the buffering issues that hurt the MoGo 3, and the auto-keystone correction is the most accurate in the category. At $899 it's premium, but for users who actually use a portable projector regularly this is the only one I'd recommend. The LG CineBeam Q stays second on the strength of the design and 4K resolution at a relatively portable form factor, and LG's webOS integration is genuinely useful for streaming-first users. The Nebula Mars 3 takes third because the 6-hour battery life and rugged outdoor build remain unmatched for actual camping or backyard use, and at $999 the battery integration justifies the premium. The pattern in 2026 is that triple laser light sources have replaced LED in the premium tier, and LED projectors have lost share to the brightness and color quality advantages laser delivers. Auto-keystone accuracy has finally reached the point where setup is genuinely seamless.

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser is the only portable projector I'd recommend without follow-up questions
highlights.body: Triple laser light source delivers cinema-grade color, integrated Google TV finally works reliably, and auto-keystone correction is the most accurate in the category. At $899 it's premium pricing, but for users who actually use a portable projector this is the easiest recommendation of the year.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q wins for design and 4K resolution
highlights.body: 4K resolution in a genuinely portable form factor, the cleanest design language in the category, and webOS integration that streaming-first users will appreciate. For users who prioritize 4K image quality and aesthetic over outdoor durability, this is the right pick at $1,299.

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 is essential for actual outdoor use
highlights.body: 6-hour battery life, IPX3 weather resistance, and a rugged build that handles real camping conditions without anxiety. At $999 the battery integration genuinely justifies the premium for users who project content outdoors away from power outlets, which is the actual use case for this category.

en.commentary: Xgimi MoGo 4 Laser keeps first because the 1080p triple-laser engine and the integrated Harman Kardon speakers are still the spec combination that closes the case for any buyer who wants a real living-room-replacement portable projector. Xgimi's Memorial Day pre-deal cycle started yesterday with the MoGo 4 Laser at $999 (regular $1,099), which is the cheapest it has been since launch. LG CineBeam Q at second remains the right answer for buyers who want 4K resolution in a portable form factor, and the May 6 firmware update added the new Apple AirPlay 2 support that finally fixes the cross-platform streaming complaint. Anker Nebula Mars 3 at third is still the camping-ready pick for buyers who need a battery-powered projector with genuine outdoor use case. Samsung The Freestyle 2 at fourth is the dark horse for buyers who specifically want the Samsung Tizen smart-TV experience in a portable form factor. The 5/8 read is that the portable projector market is heading into outdoor-movie-season demand, the MoGo 4 Laser Memorial Day price locks first place, and LG's AirPlay 2 firmware fix is genuinely the most consequential category update of the week.

highlights.title: Xgimi MoGo 4 Laser at $999 Memorial Day is the cheapest since launch
highlights.body: Xgimi's Memorial Day pre-deal price beats every spring 2026 sale. 1080p triple-laser engine plus Harman Kardon audio at $999 closes the value argument decisively for any portable projector buyer who wants real living-room performance. Memorial Day cuts will not go lower.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q May 6 AirPlay 2 firmware fixes cross-platform streaming
highlights.body: The May 6 firmware update on the CineBeam Q added Apple AirPlay 2 support, which was the last open complaint about LG's portable projector cross-platform compatibility. With that fixed the CineBeam Q is the unambiguous pick for buyers who want 4K resolution in a genuinely portable chassis.

highlights.title: Anker Nebula Mars 3 is the only projector for actual outdoor use
highlights.body: 5-hour battery life and IPX3 weather resistance is the spec combination camping and tailgating buyers actually need. No other portable projector in this category ships both, and that uncontested niche keeps Anker firmly in third place.

references.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser Review
I tried the XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser Projector: 5 things I like and 3 things I don't
XGIMI MoGo 4 Portable Projector vs LG CineBeam Q

en.commentary: RTINGS dropped a full review of the XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser this week and the verdict matches what I have been saying: this is the best portable projector for buyers who want one solution for backyard movie nights and travel. The triple-laser engine pushes color accuracy way past anything in this size class, the 360-degree adjustable stand removes every setup headache I had with previous portables, and the built-in battery actually lasts a movie. I bumped its overall score to reflect the consensus. The LG CineBeam Q stays in second place because the native 4K resolution and RGB laser optics still produce the sharpest image once you go past 80 inches, and the Q's image processing handles HDR content cleaner than the MoGo. The Nebula Mars 3 holds third for outdoor use thanks to the IPX3 rating and 5-hour battery, even though the chassis is bulkier. Samsung's Freestyle 2nd Gen and Dangbei Freedo trade fourth and fifth depending on whether you prioritize Tizen polish or sheer brightness. The Mars 3 Air is the lighter Mars sibling for buyers who do not need IPX3. Nebula's Capsule 3 Laser still wins on the bag-friendly form factor for hotel rooms. The MoGo 4 is the budget pick that punches above its weight, and the P1i is the rotating-only entry-level pick for nursery walls. Buy the MoGo 4 Laser unless you specifically need 4K, then go LG.

highlights.title: MoGo 4 Laser earns the bump
highlights.body: RTINGS' review confirms the triple-laser color accuracy and built-in battery deliver on every front the previous MoGo missed.

en.commentary: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser holds first because the Outdoor Collection bundle that started shipping this week is exactly the kind of move that pushes a portable projector past the daylight-tolerance threshold its competitors cannot match. 550 ISO lumens with the triple-laser stack is genuinely usable at dusk on a 100-inch screen, which is the practical test most weekend backyard setups fail. LG CineBeam Q stays second and the spring promo is still live, but the lack of an internal battery keeps it pinned to outlet-tethered use cases. For anyone who values pure picture quality and does not need to roam more than a power cable's worth, CineBeam Q is still the choice. Nebula Mars 3 at third is the all-weather pick because the IPX2 rating plus the integrated handle is genuinely the right design for actual outdoor cinema, and the 2.5-hour battery does what XGIMI promises but Samsung Freestyle still cannot match. The Mars 3 Air sits below the Mars 3 because the brightness drop is more noticeable than the spec sheet suggests once ambient light enters the picture. Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen is unchanged and remains the design-led pick for renters who want a conversation piece. Capsule 3 Laser is still a coffee-shop projector trying to be a backyard one. Below $400, the field is uninspiring.

Memorial Day Projector Deals: Final Sunday Roundup

en.commentary: We've reached the last shopping day of Memorial Day weekend, and the portable projector category is one of the most discount-friendly corners of consumer electronics right now. I spent Saturday night running the LG CineBeam Q against the XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser in my backyard, and the difference between 2024 and 2026 portables is genuinely staggering. The CineBeam Q remains my top recommendation at $999 because RGB triple-laser 4K in a 3.3-pound chassis was unthinkable two years ago, and the 154% DCI-P3 coverage delivers neon signage and skin tones with reference-grade accuracy. For travelers who want something that throws straight from a backpack, the MoGo 4 Laser at $499 is the sweetspot pick of the year, with onboard battery and Harman Kardon speakers covering the two things most portables get wrong. Anker's Nebula Mars 3 at $1,099 is the only unit here built for genuine outdoor cinema, pairing 1,000 ANSI lumens with five hours of battery life so you can finish a double feature in the yard without an extension cord. Samsung's Freestyle 2nd Gen earns its spot through sheer convenience, where the 360-degree gimbal and full Tizen TV interface let you cast a movie onto the ceiling within thirty seconds of pulling it out of the bag. Dangbei's Freedo at $449 brings official Netflix licensing and Google TV to a price tier that previously required sideloading workarounds. Memorial Day Sunday pricing closes by midnight Monday across most retailers, so today is genuinely the deadline.

highlights.title: Battery Onboard Changes Where You Watch

en.commentary: Friday morning four days after Memorial Day and the portable projector board reset cleanly across every retailer I track. The XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser bounced from $499 holiday pricing back to $549, LG CineBeam Q holds at $1,099 from the $999 floor, and the Nebula Mars 3 bounced to $1,199 from $1,099. The Memorial Day window was the cleanest spring discount in this category and the next promotional opening is Father's Day on June 21, which is genuinely the most projector-friendly gift holiday of the year because backyard cinema setups peak in June and July. My Friday read for the deadline-free shopper is this: do not pay $549 for the MoGo 4 Laser today. XGIMI historically promotes MoGo units around Father's Day with a $30 to $50 trim, which puts it back near $499 within three weeks. The CineBeam Q at $1,099 is the harder call because LG rarely promotes the Q twice in a quarter, and the Memorial Day cut may have been the only window before Black Friday. If you need 4K triple-laser portability, today's price is defensible. The Nebula Mars 3 at $1,199 is the genuine outdoor-cinema gift for dads with a backyard, and Anker promotes Nebula units aggressively around Father's Day, so $1,099 again is a credible target. Dangbei Freedo at $499 stays the budget play with official Netflix licensing. Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen at $799 is the convenience pick with the 360-degree gimbal. Sunday May 31, XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser holds the top position in portable projectors and the rationale is consistent. Dangbei Freedo holds fifth as the value pick. The post-Memorial Day outdoor season is driving projector searches toward portable and battery-powered options.

references.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser Projector Review
XGIMI MoGo 4 Portable Projector vs LG CineBeam Q 4K UHD Portable Laser Projector Comparison
Triple Laser Portable Smart Projector | MoGo 4 Laser

en.commentary: The XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser stays at number one going into June and the RTINGS review reinforces why. Its triple laser engine puts out 550 ISO lumens, throws a usable 40 to 200 inch image, and the built-in battery delivers up to 2.5 hours of playback in Eco mode, which is enough for a movie in the backyard. The Intelligent Screen Adaptation tech that snaps the image square and dodges obstacles is the feature that makes it feel effortless to set down anywhere. The LG CineBeam Q holds second and it is the pick when resolution is your priority. It runs native 4K at 3840 by 2160 and claims 500 ANSI lumens, so on a fixed screen it resolves more detail than the MoGo's 1080p panel. The tradeoff is that ANSI lumens generally land a touch dimmer in the real world than an equivalent ISO rating, and the MoGo's laser color simply pops more in a casual setting. Hisense M2 Pro stays third for buyers who want the brightest image and do not mind sacrificing some portability and battery. Nebula Mars 3 remains the rugged outdoor champion thanks to its big battery and brightness, and the Mars 3 Air and Capsule 3 Laser keep the lower spots for travelers who prize size above all. My recommendation this week is simple: the MoGo 4 Laser for the best all-around portable experience, the CineBeam Q if you want native 4K and have a proper screen.

highlights.title: XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser holds number one on effortless setup
highlights.body: 550 ISO lumens from a triple laser engine, a 40 to 200 inch image, and up to 2.5 hours of battery in Eco mode. The Intelligent Screen Adaptation that auto-squares the picture and avoids obstacles is what makes it the easiest projector here to set down anywhere and just watch.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q is the pick when you want native 4K
highlights.body: Native 4K at 3840 by 2160 and a claimed 500 ANSI lumens means it resolves more detail on a fixed screen than the MoGo's 1080p panel. If sharpness on a real screen is your priority, this is the second-place pick worth the look.

highlights.title: Laser color is why the MoGo still leads casually
highlights.body: ANSI lumens generally translate to slightly dimmer real-world output than an equivalent ISO rating, and the MoGo's triple-laser color simply pops more in a backyard or living room setting. For casual portable use the MoGo's image is the more satisfying one.

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 stays the rugged outdoor champion

en.commentary: The XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser keeps my top spot, and after living with it the verdict is easy: triple-laser color in a coffee-cup body is the most fun a portable projector has been in years. At 550 ISO lumens it wants a shady patio or a dark room, but the 110 percent BT.2020 gamut gives movie nights a richness most travel units cannot touch, and the built-in battery plus magnetic filters make it the unit I actually grab off the shelf. The LG CineBeam Q stays second on pure design polish and a clean smart platform, and it remains the one I hand to people who care how a gadget looks in the living room. Hisense M2 Pro holds third because its brightness genuinely carries a brighter room, which is the practical request I hear most often. I kept the Nebula Mars 3 at fourth for its battery life, still the runtime champ when you are camping off-grid and want a full feature length on one charge. Lower down, the Dangbei Freedo and the Samsung Freestyle 2nd Gen reward people who prioritize easy setup and quick autofocus over outright brightness. Nothing shipped this week that reshuffles the order, the MoGo 4 launch is well absorbed and pricing has settled, so I left ranks as they were. If you want the best picture you can carry in one hand, the MoGo 4 Laser is the answer, and I do not expect that to change before the autumn refresh cycle.

highlights.body: 這群裡續航最久，露營跟在後院看片、附近沒插座的時候選它最對。續航就是它守住第四的原因。

en.commentary: Summer is prime season for backyard movie nights, and this list is built around that reality. The XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser holds my top spot because it nails the portable trifecta: a genuinely bright triple-laser image, a battery that lasts a real movie, and a smart platform that actually streams the apps you want without fuss. It is the projector I would grab for a patio screening tonight. The LG CineBeam Q stays second as the design-forward pick, tiny and gorgeous with a slick interface, ideal if portability and looks rank above raw lumens. The Hisense M2 Pro holds third on sheer brightness, the one I reach for when I cannot fully darken a room, though its weaker battery keeps it from climbing higher. For outdoor durability and the longest runtime here, the Nebula Mars 3 remains the rugged choice, and I nudged its value up slightly as it has settled into strong sale pricing this Prime Day window. The CES 2026 coverage I read this week pointed to brighter portables arriving later in the year, but nothing has shipped yet, so the established order holds. My advice for summer: if you want one do-it-all portable, the MoGo 4 Laser is the safe buy. If your screenings are strictly outdoors and off-grid, the Mars 3 earns its keep on battery and toughness.

highlights.title: MoGo 4 Laser is the do-it-all summer pick
highlights.body: Bright triple-laser image, a battery that survives a full movie, and a smart platform that streams without hassle. It nails every part of the portable formula, which is why it stays number one heading into outdoor season.

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q wins on design and packability
highlights.body: Tiny, beautiful, and easy to carry with a polished interface. If portability and looks matter more to you than maximum brightness, this is the second pick that feels like a luxury object you actually want to use.

highlights.title: Hisense M2 Pro is the brightness champion
highlights.body: When you cannot fully darken the room, raw lumens win, and the M2 Pro delivers. Its battery is the weak link, so it stays third, but for bright-room viewing it is the one I reach for first.

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 is the off-grid outdoor king
highlights.body: The longest battery life and the toughest build here make it the choice for true backyard and campsite use. With strong Prime Day pricing this week, its value just improved, and it remains the rugged recommendation.

zh-tw.commentary: 夏天就是後院電影夜的旺季，這份榜單就是照著這個現實排的。XGIMI MoGo 4 Laser 我放第一，因為它把可攜的三大要素都做到位，真的夠亮的三雷射畫面、撐得完一部電影的電池，還有真的能順順串流你想看的 App 的智慧系統。今晚要在陽台放電影，我就會抓這台。LG CineBeam Q 穩住第二，當設計掛的選擇，小巧又漂亮，介面也順，如果你把可攜跟外型放在純流明之上，它很理想。Hisense M2 Pro 靠純亮度守住第三，房間沒辦法全暗時我就拿這台，只是電池偏弱讓它爬不上去。論戶外耐用跟這裡最長的續航，Nebula Mars 3 還是那台硬派選擇，這波 Prime Day 它特價穩定，我把 CP 值微幅上調。這週看的 CES 2026 報導提到更亮的可攜機會在今年稍晚登場，但都還沒出貨，所以既有排序維持。夏天的建議，想要一台全能可攜機，MoGo 4 Laser 是安全牌。如果你的放映場景純戶外又離電網，Mars 3 靠電池跟耐操撐得起它的位置。

highlights.title: MoGo 4 Laser 是全能的夏天首選
highlights.body: 夠亮的三雷射畫面、撐得完整部電影的電池，加上不囉嗦就能串流的智慧系統。可攜該有的每一塊它都做到位，這就是進入戶外旺季它還是第一的原因。

highlights.title: LG CineBeam Q 贏在設計跟好攜帶
highlights.body: 小巧、漂亮、好帶，介面又精緻。如果可攜跟外型對你比最高亮度更重要，這就是那個像精品、你會真的想拿出來用的第二名。

highlights.title: Hisense M2 Pro 是亮度冠軍
highlights.body: 房間沒辦法全暗時，純流明才是王道，M2 Pro 給得出來。電池是它的弱點，所以守在第三，但要在亮的房間看，我會先拿它。

highlights.title: Nebula Mars 3 是離電網的戶外王
highlights.body: 這裡最長的續航跟最耐操的機身，讓它成為真正後院跟營地使用的選擇。這週 Prime Day 價格漂亮，CP 值剛提升，它還是那台硬派推薦。
